mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate increases discount rates for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness forces wage inflation that erodes corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE drops below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates demand fragility that triggers rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts for two consecutive months while housing starts remain above 1.0 million units, supply chain bottlenecks force equity market corrections over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < 0 for 2 months and HOUST > 1000.0
failure_condition: The trigger condition is associated with positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of historical instances.
